agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "New 'Express Delivery' product offering time-sensitive delivery with a target 2-hour window, premium pricing, and conditional refund policy. Cross-functional stakeholders have provided conflicting requirements around the nature of the delivery promise (guarantee vs. best effort), refund triggers, and operational constraints.",
    "bounded_context": "Express Delivery — encompasses order eligibility, express fulfillment lifecycle, driver assignment, SLA tracking, and express-specific refund processing. Sits adjacent to existing Standard Delivery, Driver Management, Warehouse/Inventory, and Billing contexts.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Express Delivery", "definition": "A premium delivery option targeting a 2-hour delivery window from order placement to doorstep, available to eligible customers for an additional fee." },
      { "term": "Express Fee", "definition": "The premium surcharge ($12.99) charged on top of the base delivery fee for express service." },
      { "term": "Base Delivery Fee", "definition": "The standard delivery charge that applies to all orders regardless of delivery speed. Never refunded under express SLA miss." },
      { "term": "Express Window", "definition": "The 2-hour target timeframe beginning at order placement within which delivery should be completed." },
      { "term": "Pickup SLA", "definition": "The 15-minute maximum time from order placement to physical pickup by a driver from the warehouse. Exceeding this jeopardizes the express window." },
      { "term": "Express-Eligible Driver", "definition": "A driver with a rating of 4.0 stars or above who is qualified to fulfill express orders." },
      { "term": "Service Radius", "definition": "The 10km maximum distance between the customer's delivery address and the nearest warehouse, determining express eligibility." },
      { "term": "SLA Miss", "definition": "When delivery is not completed within the express window. Triggers a conditional goodwill refund of the express fee." },
      { "term": "Goodwill Refund", "definition": "Refund of the express fee issued as a business courtesy when the SLA is missed, subject to exclusions (e.g., wrong address). Not a contractual obligation." },
      { "term": "Driver Reassignment", "definition": "The process of assigning a replacement express-eligible driver when the original driver cancels mid-delivery. Must occur within 10 minutes." },
      { "term": "Wrong Address", "definition": "A delivery address provided by the customer that is materially incorrect, undeliverable, or does not match the customer's intent. Disqualifies the order from goodwill refund eligibility. DEFINITION IS CURRENTLY AMBIGUOUS — see escalations." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A customer may only select Express Delivery if their delivery address is within 10km of an active warehouse.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Physical constraint — express fulfillment is impossible if no warehouse is within serviceable range. Allowing orders outside this radius would create undeliverable commitments.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a delivery address more than 10km from any warehouse, the system MUST NOT allow express delivery selection."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "Express orders must be picked up by an express-eligible driver within 15 minutes of order placement.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational constraint tied to the physics of the 2-hour window. If pickup exceeds 15 minutes, the remaining transit time becomes insufficient to meet the SLA in most scenarios.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order placed at time T, an express-eligible driver must have physically picked up the package by T+15 minutes, or the system must trigger an escalation workflow."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "Only drivers with a rating of 4.0 stars or above may be assigned to express deliveries.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Business policy threshold. Operations could adjust the minimum rating (e.g., to 3.5 during driver shortages) without corrupting system state.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver with rating below 4.0 stars, the system MUST NOT assign them to an express order."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "The express fee is $12.99, charged in addition to the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Pricing policy that can be changed by business decision. Not a system integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order, the total delivery charge includes the base delivery fee plus exactly $12.99 express surcharge."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "When the express window is missed, only the express fee is eligible for refund — never the base delivery fee.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Core financial invariant. The base delivery fee covers the cost of the delivery itself, which was still performed. Refunding it would mean providing free delivery, breaking the unit economics model.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an SLA miss refund, the refund amount MUST equal exactly the express fee. The base delivery fee MUST remain charged."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "No goodwill refund is issued if the customer provided a wrong address, regardless of whether the SLA was missed.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Business policy to prevent abuse. The definition of 'wrong address' and the exclusion itself are policy decisions that could be revised.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an SLA miss where the order is flagged as 'wrong address provided by customer', the system MUST NOT auto-process a goodwill refund."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-7",
      "rule": "Goodwill refunds for missed express windows are processed within 3 business days.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational SLA for the finance team. Could be shortened or lengthened by policy change.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a qualified SLA miss, the refund must be initiated within 3 business days of the delivery completion timestamp."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-8",
      "rule": "If a driver cancels mid-express-delivery, a replacement express-eligible driver must be assigned within 10 minutes. If reassignment fails, a goodwill refund is automatically triggered.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Operational policy and customer promise. The 10-minute window and auto-refund trigger are tunable business decisions.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given a driver cancellation on an active express delivery at time T, either a replacement driver is assigned by T+10 minutes, or the system automatically initiates a goodwill refund of the express fee."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-9",
      "rule": "Express delivery customers must have access to real-time driver tracking from pickup through delivery.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Product feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint. Could be degraded gracefully without system corruption.",
      "testable_assertion": "Given an express order in transit, the customer-facing interface must display the driver's current location, updated at a frequency sufficient for 'real-time' perception."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "No express-eligible drivers available within the service radius at time of order", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — should the system block express selection, queue the order, or downgrade to standard? Must be defined." },
    { "scenario": "Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse (boundary condition)", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — is 10km inclusive or exclusive? Is it straight-line (geodesic) distance or driving/routing distance?" },
    { "scenario": "Pickup SLA (15 min) is missed but delivery still completed within the 2-hour window", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — Operations says this jeopardizes the window, but if delivery succeeds, is there any consequence? Should this trigger an internal escalation regardless?" },
    { "scenario": "Driver cancels at minute 110 of the 2-hour window (near SLA boundary)", "expected_behavior": "Reassignment within 10 minutes is nearly impossible to result in on-time delivery. Should auto-refund trigger immediately instead of waiting 10 minutes?" },
    { "scenario": "Customer changes address after order placement to a location outside the 10km radius", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — should this cancel express status? Downgrade to standard? Block the address change?" },
    { "scenario": "Multiple warehouse proximity — customer is within 10km of two warehouses", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — which warehouse is selected? Closest? Most inventory? Most available drivers?" },
    { "scenario": "Partial wrong address — building is correct but apartment number is wrong", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does 'wrong address' mean completely undeliverable, or any inaccuracy? Who adjudicates?" },
    { "scenario": "Express delivery during severe weather or system outage", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — should express be disabled proactively? Is there a force majeure exclusion?" },
    { "scenario": "Replacement driver also cancels within the 10-minute reassignment window", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does the 10-minute clock reset or is the auto-refund triggered immediately?" },
    { "scenario": "Customer is unreachable/unavailable at delivery time within the 2-hour window", "expected_behavior": "UNDEFINED — does waiting for the customer count against the SLA? At what point is the delivery considered 'attempted'?" }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Express fee ($12.99) creates a new premium revenue stream. However, the goodwill refund policy introduces refund exposure on every express order. The auto-refund on failed driver reassignment (BR-8) could be exploited or cascade during peak times when driver supply is thin. Without clear 'wrong address' adjudication, refund disputes will increase operational costs.",
    "operational": "The 15-minute pickup SLA and 4-star driver requirement severely constrain the eligible driver pool. During peak hours or in sparse coverage areas, this could create a bottleneck where express is offered but operationally undeliverable. Real-time tracking adds infrastructure cost and reliability requirements. The 10-minute driver reassignment SLA requires a robust, always-available dispatch system.",
    "user": "Express delivery is a strong value proposition for time-sensitive customers. However, the contradiction between 'guaranteed' (PM/Customer Success language) and 'best effort' (Legal) creates a trust risk. If marketing says 'guaranteed' but Terms of Service say 'best effort,' customer satisfaction will suffer when refunds are denied. Prominent display of the 2-hour window (Customer Success request) combined with Legal's position could expose brand risk.",
    "risk": "PRIMARY RISK: The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is a litigation and brand risk. Secondary: the 'wrong address' exclusion without a clear adjudication process will generate customer complaints and inconsistent support decisions. Tertiary: if the 15-minute pickup SLA is frequently missed, the product's core promise erodes and refund costs spiral.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers", "impact": "Gain a premium fast-delivery option but may be misled by 'guarantee' language. Refund exclusions (wrong address) lack transparency." },
      { "group": "Drivers", "impact": "Higher-rated drivers get more express assignments (higher earnings potential). Creates a two-tier driver economy. Cancellation penalties unclear." },
      { "group": "Warehouse Operations", "impact": "15-minute pickup SLA imposes hard time pressure on picking and packing. May require dedicated express staging areas." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Will handle refund disputes, 'wrong address' adjudication, and complaints about missed SLAs. Need clear runbooks and decision authority." },
      { "group": "Finance/Billing", "impact": "Must build refund processing within 3-business-day SLA. Need to handle auto-refunds from failed reassignment alongside manual refund review." },
      { "group": "Legal/Compliance", "impact": "Must ensure customer-facing language aligns with 'best effort' positioning. Refund described as 'goodwill' in ToS, not entitlement." },
      { "group": "Product/Marketing", "impact": "Must reconcile 'guaranteed' product messaging with Legal's 'best effort' constraint. Misalignment here is the highest-priority issue." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 35,
    "reasoning": "Confidence is very low due to a fundamental contradiction at the core of the product: the PM and Customer Success describe a 'guarantee' while Legal explicitly prohibits calling it a guarantee. This is not a minor wording issue — it affects the refund policy, customer-facing UX, acceptance criteria, and the entire product positioning. Until this contradiction is resolved, acceptance criteria cannot be reliably defined. Additionally, multiple key terms ('wrong address', distance measurement method, escalation behavior on pickup SLA miss) are undefined.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "Express fee amount ($12.99) is agreed upon across stakeholders",
      "Base delivery fee is never refunded — all parties align",
      "10km service radius as an eligibility constraint is clear in intent",
      "4-star minimum driver rating is a clear operational policy",
      "15-minute pickup SLA is a clear operational constraint"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether the 2-hour window is a guarantee or best-effort SLA — fundamental contradiction",
      "Refund trigger: automatic vs. manual vs. hybrid — stakeholders disagree",
      "Definition and adjudication of 'wrong address'",
      "Behavior when pickup SLA is missed but delivery window is not",
      "Distance measurement method (straight-line vs. driving distance)",
      "Express availability hours and force majeure exclusions",
      "What happens when no eligible drivers are available"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The 10km radius is measured from the customer's delivery address to the nearest warehouse, not the driver's current location",
      "The 2-hour window starts at order placement, not at pickup",
      "Driver rating is a cumulative/rolling average, not per-delivery",
      "Real-time tracking means GPS-based location updates visible to the customer, not just status updates",
      "The 3-business-day refund processing window is for the finance operation, not the customer-facing promise"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "contradiction",
      "description": "CRITICAL: PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery as a 'guarantee' with automatic refunds. Legal explicitly states it CANNOT be a guarantee — it is 'best effort' and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These are mutually exclusive positions. This contradiction affects every downstream decision: UX copy, refund automation, Terms of Service, and marketing. The product cannot ship until this is resolved.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Product/Marketing", "Legal/Compliance", "Customers", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A: Adopt Legal's position — 'best effort' SLA with discretionary goodwill refunds. PM and Customer Success adjust expectations and UX language accordingly.",
        "B: Adopt PM's position — contractual guarantee with automatic refunds. Legal must accept increased liability exposure and draft compliant guarantee language.",
        "C: Hybrid — market as a 'promise' (not 'guarantee'), automate refunds as standard practice but retain ToS language that refunds are goodwill. Balances customer trust with legal protection."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C (Hybrid). Use language like 'Our Express Promise' rather than 'guarantee.' Automate refunds to honor the spirit of the PM's intent while preserving Legal's non-contractual positioning. This is standard practice in logistics (e.g., Amazon's delivery promises). However, Legal MUST formally sign off on the exact language."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "The term 'wrong address' is used by Finance as a refund exclusion but is not defined. Does it mean: (a) a completely fabricated/non-existent address, (b) a typo in the street number, (c) a missing apartment/unit number, (d) an address where the customer is not physically present? Who makes the determination — the driver, an automated system, or customer support? Without a clear definition, this rule is unenforceable and will cause inconsistent refund decisions.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customer Support", "Customers", "Finance/Billing"],
      "options": [
        "A: Define narrowly — only addresses that are undeliverable (non-existent, unreachable). Partial errors (wrong apt number) still qualify for refund.",
        "B: Define broadly — any customer-caused address error, including missing details, disqualifies refund.",
        "C: Tiered approach — address validation at order time catches most errors. Only addresses that pass validation but are still wrong at delivery count."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C. Implement address validation at checkout to prevent most errors upfront. If the address passed validation but is still wrong at delivery, exclude from refund. This is fair, automatable, and reduces support disputes."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No stakeholder addressed what happens when the 15-minute pickup SLA is missed. Operations says the 2-hour window cannot be met, but there is no defined escalation: does the order get cancelled? Downgraded to standard delivery? Does the customer get notified? Is the express fee refunded preemptively? This is a high-frequency operational scenario that MUST be defined before launch.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Warehouse Operations", "Customers", "Customer Support", "Drivers"],
      "options": [
        "A: Auto-cancel express and downgrade to standard delivery with proactive customer notification and express fee refund.",
        "B: Keep as express but extend the window and notify the customer of the delay.",
        "C: Escalate to dispatch supervisor for manual decision within 5 minutes."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A. Downgrade to standard and proactively refund the express fee. The customer keeps their order, gets transparency, and the business avoids a guaranteed SLA miss. This is the cleanest operational path."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "Distance measurement method is unspecified. '10km from a warehouse' could mean straight-line (geodesic) distance or actual driving/routing distance. In urban environments with rivers, highways, or restricted zones, these can differ by 2-3x. This directly affects which customers see the express option.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Product/Marketing", "Operations"],
      "options": [
        "A: Straight-line distance — simpler to compute, but will include some customers who are unreachable within 2 hours.",
        "B: Driving distance — more accurate but requires routing API calls at eligibility check time.",
        "C: Driving distance with a buffer — use 8km driving distance to account for traffic variability."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option B. Use driving distance. The 2-hour window is tight enough that straight-line distance would create false eligibility in many urban areas, leading to SLA misses and refund costs. The routing API cost is negligible compared to the cost of failed express deliveries."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No express availability hours or force majeure policy defined. Is express available 24/7? Only during business hours? What happens during severe weather, natural disasters, or system outages? Most logistics companies suspend time-sensitive SLAs during exceptional circumstances.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers", "Operations", "Legal/Compliance"],
      "options": [
        "A: Express available only during defined operating hours (e.g., 7am-10pm) with a force majeure exclusion clause.",
        "B: Express available 24/7 with no exclusions.",
        "C: Express available 24/7 but with dynamic availability based on real-time driver supply and conditions."
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C. Dynamic availability is the most operationally sound. Only offer express when sufficient eligible drivers are available and no weather/disruption alerts are active. This prevents offering a service that cannot be fulfilled."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": [
    { "violation": "The product cannot proceed to implementation with the guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction unresolved. PM says 'guaranteed,' Legal says 'best effort.' This is not a wording issue — it determines refund automation logic, UX copy, Terms of Service, and liability exposure.", "business_rule_broken": "Fundamental product definition — no coherent SLA can be encoded when stakeholders disagree on whether it is binding." },
    { "violation": "'Wrong address' is used as a refund exclusion without any definition, adjudication process, or validation mechanism. Implementing this rule without clarity will create inconsistent customer outcomes and support escalations.", "business_rule_broken": "BR-6 — rule is unimplementable as stated." }
  ]
}
